purpose: Valibot is a modular, type-safe schema library for TypeScript/JavaScript used to validate unknown data (server inputs, forms, config files) at runtime with static type inference. Designed for tiny bundle size (starts <700 bytes) via tree-shakeable function-based API.

public_api.name: object
public_api.summary: Creates a schema describing a structured object with typed keys.

public_api.name: string
public_api.summary: Schema that validates string values.

public_api.name: number
public_api.summary: Schema that validates number values.

public_api.name: boolean
public_api.summary: Schema that validates boolean values.

public_api.name: array
public_api.summary: Schema for arrays of a given item schema.

public_api.name: pipe
public_api.summary: Composes a schema with validation/transformation actions (e.g., email(), minLength()).

public_api.name: parse
public_api.summary: Parses input against schema; throws ValiError on failure.

public_api.name: safeParse
public_api.summary: Non-throwing variant of parse returning a result object.

public_api.name: is
public_api.summary: Type guard that returns whether input matches the schema.

public_api.name: parser
public_api.summary: Creates a reusable parse function bound to a schema.

public_api.name: safeParser
public_api.summary: Creates a reusable safeParse function.

public_api.name: email
public_api.summary: Pipe action validating email format on strings.

public_api.name: minLength
public_api.summary: Pipe action enforcing minimum length.

public_api.name: maxLength
public_api.summary: Pipe action enforcing maximum length.

public_api.name: optional
public_api.summary: Marks a schema as optional (may be undefined).

public_api.name: nullable
public_api.summary: Allows null in addition to the wrapped schema.

public_api.name: nullish
public_api.summary: Allows null or undefined in addition to the wrapped schema.

public_api.name: union
public_api.summary: Schema matching any of the provided schemas.

public_api.name: intersect
public_api.summary: Schema matching all of the provided schemas (intersection).

public_api.name: picklist
public_api.summary: Schema matching one of a list of literal values.

public_api.name: enum
public_api.summary: Schema matching values from a TypeScript enum.

public_api.name: literal
public_api.summary: Schema matching a single literal value.

public_api.name: record
public_api.summary: Schema for objects with dynamic keys.

public_api.name: tuple
public_api.summary: Schema for fixed-length tuples.

public_api.name: lazy
public_api.summary: Schema resolved lazily for recursive types.

public_api.name: transform
public_api.summary: Pipe action that transforms parsed value.

public_api.name: check
public_api.summary: Pipe action that runs a custom predicate check.

public_api.name: brand
public_api.summary: Adds a nominal brand to a schema's output type.

public_api.name: fallback
public_api.summary: Provides a fallback value when validation fails.

public_api.name: parseAsync
public_api.summary: Async variant of parse for async schemas/actions.

public_api.name: safeParseAsync
public_api.summary: Async variant of safeParse.

public_api.name: InferOutput
public_api.summary: Type helper to infer the output TypeScript type of a schema.

public_api.name: InferInput
public_api.summary: Type helper to infer the input TypeScript type of a schema.

public_api.name: InferIssue
public_api.summary: Type helper to infer issue types produced by a schema.

public_api.name: ValiError
public_api.summary: Error thrown by parse on validation failure; contains issues.

public_api.name: flatten
public_api.summary: Flattens issues into a simpler error structure.

public_api.name: getDotPath
public_api.summary: Returns a dot-path string for an issue.

usage_patterns.title: Define and parse a schema
usage_patterns.body: Import everything as `v` from 'valibot'. Build a schema with constructors like v.object, v.string, v.number. Add validations via v.pipe(schema, v.email(), v.minLength(8)). Then run v.parse(schema, input) to validate (throws ValiError) or v.safeParse for a result object.

usage_patterns.title: Type inference
usage_patterns.body: Use `type T = v.InferOutput<typeof Schema>` to get the TypeScript type that a schema produces. InferInput gives the pre-transform type, InferIssue gives issue union types.

usage_patterns.title: Type guarding
usage_patterns.body: Use v.is(schema, input) as a TypeScript type guard — returns boolean and narrows input to InferOutput<typeof schema>.

usage_patterns.title: Async validation
usage_patterns.body: Use *Async variants (objectAsync, pipeAsync, checkAsync, parseAsync, safeParseAsync) when any part of validation requires Promises, e.g., checking database uniqueness.

usage_patterns.title: Modular imports for small bundles
usage_patterns.body: The API is composed of many small independent functions; bundlers tree-shake unused functions. Import only what you need to keep bundles minimal (announced as small as ~700 bytes for tiny schemas).

gotchas: parse throws a ValiError on failure; use safeParse if you want a non-exception result.
Validation/transformation actions (email, minLength, etc.) must be composed via v.pipe, not chained as methods — Valibot has no fluent builder API.
Async actions require the *Async schema constructors and parseAsync/safeParseAsync; mixing sync parse with async actions will not work.
Breaking API changes occurred at v0.31.0 — older tutorials may use a different API; see the 'To v0.31.0' migration guide.
Zod schemas are not drop-in compatible; use the 'From Zod' migration guide when porting.

examples.title: Login schema with parse
